_meta.purpose: Mimic a real Trae 1.x install's hook payload shape (UNVERIFIED — see slice #2 closeout code-review M-1).
_meta.traeVersion: 1.x (assumed; no live install available — see slice 009 PRD R-1)
_meta.shapeSource: Trae documented as a 'Cursor sibling' per slice #2; Cursor uses eventName+parameters envelope. We assume Trae 1.x follows the same shape until a real install is dogfooded.
_meta.usage: tests/integration/ide/trae-1x.test.ts and tests/unit/ide/trae-1x-adapter.test.ts load this fixture to pin the hookEvent, toolMatcher, settingsFileName, and TRAE_DENY_SHAPE values.

settingsJson._comment: Settings file that a real Trae 1.x install would pre-populate. peaks's `peaks hooks install` MUST preserve these fields (round-trip case).

hookStdinDenyPath._comment: Real Trae 1.x is assumed to send { eventName, parameters: { command } } when a hook fires before a tool call. peaks parses this in parseTraeShapeStdin (src/services/ide/hook-translator.ts).

hookStdinAllowPath._comment: Allow-path payload: a benign command that should not be denied by peaks gate enforce.

hookStdinEmpty._comment: Empty / no-payload case: peak hook handle must fail-open to allow.

hookStdinTerminal._comment: Real Trae 1.x is assumed to surface the tool name in `parameters.tool` (or similar). peaks currently uses eventName as toolName since Trae has not been observed to carry a top-level tool field. If a future slice finds `parameters.tool: 'terminal'`, parseTraeShapeStdin should be updated to extract that field.

expectedAdapterValues._comment: The peaks Trae adapter's 4 UNVERIFIED fields. The slice 009 dogfood classifies each as VERIFIED-AS-IS / VERIFIED-WITH-CORRECTION / VERIFIED-AS-UNSUPPORTED against the fixture shape above. See .peaks/_runtime/2026-06-06-session-5b1095/qa/dogfood-trae-1x-2026-06-07.md for the resolution table.
